evals.prompt: Create an MCP server with a tool to fetch GitHub repository information.
evals.expected: The skill asks the user which language they prefer and shows the 9-language menu before generating any code.
assertions: Response shows a numbered list of available languages (TypeScript, Python, C#, Go, Java, Rust, Swift, Ruby, PHP)
Response does NOT generate any code before the language is confirmed
Response mentions Tier 1 or highlights TypeScript as the default
Response asks what the server should expose (tools/resources/prompts)
Response does not assume TypeScript without asking

evals.prompt: Create a TypeScript MCP server with a tool to search GitHub repositories by keyword. Include tests.
evals.expected: Generates a properly structured TypeScript MCP project with separated concerns and both unit + integration tests.
assertions: Generates src/index.ts as the entry point with no business logic
Generates src/tools/github.ts with a registerGithubTools(server) export
Generates src/lib/github-service.ts with pure business logic and zero MCP SDK imports
Generates src/lib/types.ts or co-locates Zod schemas — schemas are NOT duplicated
Generates tests/unit/lib/github-service.test.ts that tests the service with a mocked HttpClient
Generates tests/integration/tools/github.test.ts using InMemoryTransport
All tool handler errors are caught and returned as { isError: true } — no throws
package.json includes 'type': 'module' and vitest as dev dependency
Zod .describe() is present on every input schema field

evals.prompt: I want to build a Python MCP server that connects to a PostgreSQL database and exposes a query tool.
evals.expected: Generates a Python MCP server with FastMCP, separated service layer, and pytest tests.
assertions: Uses FastMCP, not a raw Server class
Business logic is in a separate lib/ module with no MCP imports
Tool registration is in a separate tools/ module
Tests use pytest and pytest-anyio
Unit tests mock the database client — no real DB connection required
Integration tests use create_connected_server_and_client_session or equivalent
Error handling returns structured error responses, not raised exceptions

evals.prompt: Create a TypeScript MCP tool that logs debug information during execution.
evals.expected: Uses server.sendLoggingMessage() or stderr — never console.log — for debug output.
assertions: Does not use console.log() in any tool handler
Uses server.sendLoggingMessage() or process.stderr.write() for debug output
Includes a comment or note explaining why console.log corrupts stdio transport

evals.prompt: Create a TypeScript MCP tool that calls an external API and handles failures.
evals.expected: Tool handler catches all errors and returns { isError: true } — never throws.
assertions: Tool handler body is wrapped in try/catch
Catch block returns { isError: true, content: [{ type: 'text', text: message }] }
A shared handleToolError() utility is used or generated in lib/errors.ts
No throw statement exits the tool handler
